'''Lee Gaskell''' (born 28 October 1990) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer plays as a {{rlp|so|fb}} or {{rlp|ce}} for [[Hunslet RLFC|Hunslet]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref name="Player Profile">{{cite web|url=http://saintsrlfc.com/player/96|title=Player Profile|publisher=sthelensrlfc.com|date=2009-11-23|accessdate=2009-11-23}}</ref>

He has also played in the [[Super League]] for [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] and spent time on loan from [[St Helens R.F.C.|Saints]] at the [[Salford Red Devils]]. He played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the Super League and in the [[Kingstone Press Championship]].

==Background==
Gaskell was born in [[St Helens, Merseyside|St Helens]], Merseyside, England.

He started his career with amateur club Blackbrook Royals.<ref name="Player Profile" />

==Playing career==
===St Helens===
Gaskell made his Super League début for St Helens in a loss at home to [[Catalans Dragons]] in round 21 of 2010's [[Super League XV]]. He started the match at stand-off and scored a try.<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.sthelensstar.co.uk/sport/8266273.print|title=Saints 20 Catalans 30 |publisher=St Helens Star|date=11 July 2010|accessdate=21 February 2014}}</ref>

He played in the [[2011 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2011/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-match-report?newsfeed=true|title=Leeds claim Grand Final glory as inspired Rob Burrow sinks St Helens|date=8 October 2011|work=Guardian|accessdate=21 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/8815944/St-Helens-16-Leeds-32-match-report.html|title=St Helens 16 Leeds 32|date=8 October 2011|work=Daily Telegraph|accessdate=21 November 2019}}</ref>

In 2013, Gaskell moved to Salford as part of a loan deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/21968435|title=St. Helens' Gaskell and Salford's Howarth in loan swap|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|date=28 March 2013|access-date=21 February 2014}}</ref>

Near the end of the 2013 season, [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] announced that they had signed Gaskell on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/23879192|title=Bradford sign St Helens stand-off Lee Gaskell|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|date=29 August 2013|access-date=21 February 2014}}</ref> In November 2016, he signed a four-year deal with [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].<ref>{{cite news|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12214/10657019/lee-gaskell-leaves-bradford-for-huddersfield-on-four-year-deal|title=Lee Gaskell leaves Bradford for Huddersfield on four-year deal|work=Sky Sports|date=14 November 2016|accessdate=20 February 2017}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
Gaskell featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Hull F.C.|Hull FC]] and [[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]].

Gaskell featured in Round 1 ([[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]) to Round 2 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]). After returning from injury he played in Round 8 ([[Salford Red Devils|Salford]]) to Round 26 ([[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]]). In the [[Challenge Cup]] he featured in Round 5 ([[Catalans Dragons]]) and the Quarter Final ([[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]]).{{cn|date=October 2022}}

Gaskell signed a two-year extension to his contract. He featured in the pre-season friendlies against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

He featured in Round 1 ([[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]]) to Round 16 ([[Doncaster R.L.F.C.|Doncaster]]). Gaskell played in Qualifier 1 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]) to Qualifier 2 ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]) then in Qualifier 7 ([[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]]). Gaskell played in the £1 Million Game ([[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]]). He also featured in the [[Challenge Cup]] in Round 4 ([[Workington Town]]) to Round 5 ([[Hull Kingston Rovers]]).{{cn|date=October 2022}}

[[File:Lee Gaskell Huddersfield.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Gaskell playing for the Huddersfield Giants in 2019]]
Gaskell featured in the pre-season friendly against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]].

Gaskell featured in Round 1 ([[Featherstone Rovers]]) to Round 4 ([[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]]) then in Round 6 ([[Batley Bulldogs|Batley]]) to Round 10 ([[Dewsbury Rams]]). Gaskell featured in Round 13 ([[Swinton Lions]]) to Round 14 ([[Sheffield Eagles]]). Gaskell played in the [[Challenge Cup]] in the 4th Round ([[Dewsbury Rams|Dewsbury]]).

At the end of 2017, Gaskell signed a four-year deal to join Super League side [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]].{{cn|date=October 2022}}

===Huddersfield Giants===
Gaskell was rewarded with his performances for Huddersfield by winning four awards at the Giants' 2019 end of season awards ceremony. <ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/article/7063/gaskell-sweeps-giants-awards|title=Gaskell Sweeps Giants Awards}}</ref>
In round 5 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he kicked a field goal in the final second of the match as Huddersfield defeated Leeds 14-13 to claim their first win of the season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/12293586/ian-watson-huddersfield-giants-head-coach-feeling-good-after-first-super-league-win-of-2021/|title=Huddersfield Giants head coach feeling good after first Super League win of 2021|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>
The following week, he scored two tries in a 26-20 victory over [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/19313224.warrington-20-huddersfield-26-man-steel-points/|title=Warrington 20 Huddersfield 26 Man of steel points|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 28 June 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=28 Jun 2021|accessdate=24 Dec 2021|title=Lee Gaskell: Wakefield Trinity agree deal for Huddersfield Giants stand-off|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/57641479}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (rejoin)===
On 19 June 2023, it was reported that he had rejoined [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Jun 2023|accessdate=19 Jun 2023|title='Unfinished business' for Lee Gaskell as he returns to former club following Wakefield release|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/lee-gaskell-bradford-bulls-wakefield-trinity-release}}</ref>

===Hunslet RLFC===
On 18 March 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hunslet RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=18 Mar 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Former St Helens and Bradford star lands Championship deal for 2025|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-st-helens-and-bradford-star-lands-championship-deal-for-2025}}</ref>
